cityDNA.coreFantasy: Skyrend Peak is a raptorfolk cliff bastion where border walls, forges, command yards, observatories, and containment courts turn height into military-civic responsibility.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Skyrend Peak should feel immediately distinct: raptor-like avian folk civic life is organized around Fortress / Border Wall, Heavy Industry / Foundry, Military Command, Observatory / Astronomy, and Prison / Containment, with Raptor Windspire Eyrie of the First Flight as the citywide symbol.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: The city turns Granite, Slate, Cedar, Glass, Canvas, Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces, and Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Flock Sight into a working urban system rather than surface decoration.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Use Granite, Slate, Cedar, Glass, Canvas, civic terraces, public courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Military Command Hall, visible Border Wall Beacon, the central Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat, and the skyline-scale Raptor Windspire Eyrie of the First Flight as the core visual grammar.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A raptor-like avian folk population whose institutions make daily life legible through section anchors: Fortress / Border Wall, Heavy Industry / Foundry, Military Command, Observatory / Astronomy, Prison / Containment.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city is symbolized by Raptor Windspire Eyrie of the First Flight, Border Wall Beacon, Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat, and the repeating civic pattern of hall, landmark, monument, and nook in every section.

canonLocks: Skyrend Peak is built for its raptor-like avian folk population and should not be genericized into a neutral fantasy city.
The Wonder is Raptor Windspire Eyrie of the First Flight.
The Beacon is Border Wall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat.
The five section spines are Fortress / Border Wall, Heavy Industry / Foundry, Military Command, Observatory / Astronomy, Prison / Containment.
The tactical board uses 25 district slots, with District 13 as the seat-of-power center.
Exact sidecar names for civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks must be preserved verbatim.
provisionalCanon: Citizens likely treat Border Wall Beacon as the practical public signal point for citywide alerts and Conjunction response.
Daily politics likely moves through the named section institutions rather than through faction headquarters.
The city’s civic magic expresses through Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Flock Sight when visible in public spaces.
rejectedReads: Skyrend Peak as a generic fantasy settlement with the culture applied as ornament only.
Replacing exact section anchors with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, archive, or court when the sidecar provides a proper name.
Treating the Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power as interchangeable skyline props instead of civic machinery.
cautionFlags: Do not add unsupported per-district landmark names beyond the source-backed section anchors.
Keep all 25 districts, but derive their specific identity from the five section spines unless new district-specific sidecars exist.
Preserve the material language: Granite, Slate, Cedar, Glass, Canvas.
Preserve the terrain/water logic: Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces; water context: dry city ground.

environment.environmentalLogic: Environment should make the city’s institutions readable: Fortress / Border Wall, Heavy Industry / Foundry, Military Command, Observatory / Astronomy, Prison / Containment must feel embedded in Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces, with water handled as dry city ground and public magic appearing as Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Flock Sight.

skyline: Raptor Windspire Eyrie of the First Flight dominates the far skyline and should be larger than every section landmark.
Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat anchors the central District 13 civic mass.
Border Wall Beacon is the section-1 signal landmark and must be readable from nearby districts.
Section skyline rhythm: Border Wall Beacon, Great Forge, Command Bastion, Star Observatory, Containment Keep.

civilization.people: The population is raptor-like avian folk. Civic scenes should show them using the city’s architecture naturally: gathering near Border Wall Beacon, moving through Fortress / Border Wall, Heavy Industry / Foundry, Military Command, and treating the named anchors as practical institutions.
socialClasses: seat-of-power officials attached to Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat
section hall administrators and permit keepers
craft, market, transport, religious, or military guild households depending on district spine
street vendors, carriers, maintenance workers, and household service families
visitors and petitioners who navigate the city through public anchors
civilization.governance: Governance is centered on Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat and expressed locally through the five section civic halls. Authority should be visible in courts, archives, public signals, permits, and named monuments rather than hidden abstraction.
civilization.religion: Religious and symbolic life should draw on Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Flock Sight, the Wonder’s public symbolism, and whichever section anchors carry sacred or ceremonial roles.
customs: citizens use named halls and nooks as practical meeting points
public signals from the Beacon interrupt ordinary commerce and movement
section monuments preserve civic memory and local identity
visitors orient themselves by Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section major landmarks
formal petitions and judgments move toward District 13
bodyLanguage: citizens make room for official messengers near beacon approaches
locals gesture by landmark rather than by street name
public posture changes around courts, monuments, and halls
market or service workers keep one eye on signal points during tense periods
speechPatterns: street speech abbreviates section landmarks but not Wonder, Beacon, or Seat names
public announcements use practical civic language rather than poetic vagueness

streets.appearance: Street views should show Granite, Slate, Cedar, Glass, Canvas, civic terraces, public courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, pedestrians using section halls, and sightlines back to Border Wall Beacon or Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat where appropriate.
streets.movement: Movement is organized by section spine: Fortress / Border Wall, Heavy Industry / Foundry, Military Command, Observatory / Astronomy, Prison / Containment. Districts feed toward their civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook before converging on District 13 for authority scenes.
streets.lighting: Use civic blue illumination for official signal moments, practical lantern light for night scenes, and material-specific highlights on Granite, Slate, Cedar, Glass.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce terrain and water logic: Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces; water context dry city ground.
streets.dayCycle: Mornings emphasize deliveries, hall openings, street maintenance, market preparation, and official notices; afternoons build toward hearings, training, guild work, or section-specific labor.
streets.nightCycle: Evenings compress around lanterns, food stalls, guarded thresholds, quieter civic errands, and beacon visibility against darker streets.

prestigious: Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat document ateliers
Raptor Windspire Eyrie of the First Flight ceremonial suppliers

housing.wealthy: Residences near Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat or high-status section courts use the best Granite, Slate, Cedar, private terraces, and controlled views of civic landmarks.
housing.middle: Households cluster above shops and along civic streets, sharing courtyards, balconies, workrooms, and access to section halls.
housing.poor: Lower-status housing is practical, crowded, and close to service yards or labor streets, but still uses local motifs and public landmarks for orientation.

wonder.name: Raptor Windspire Eyrie of the First Flight
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of raptor-like avian folk identity made public and monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A one-per-city wonder: spectacular, unmistakable, and larger than every section landmark. It turns Skyrend Peak's geography, race identity, material culture, and civic magic into one public symbol. Built for Skyrend Peak's raptor-like avian folk population, it uses Granite, Slate, Cedar, Glass, Canvas across Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces; water context: dry city ground; magic expression: Beast Bonding, An
wonder.civicRole: A unifying civic symbol larger than section landmarks; it gives Skyrend Peak its skyline identity and public myth.
storyHooks: a public ceremony at the Wonder changes city mood
maintenance access reveals hidden civic routes
the Wonder becomes the reference point during a beacon state

beacon.name: Border Wall Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: The public signal point where citywide warning becomes local action.
beacon.visualRole: A largest local landmark in this section, subordinate only to the city wonder and citadel. It belongs to Section 1 (Fortress / Border Wall) of Skyrend Peak and should use White Limestone Civic Blocks, Obsidian Fortresses, Cyclopean Megaliths, granite and glass materials, Carved Totems, Civic Blue Glow, Woven Banners, and the raptor-like avian folk visual language. Design focus: curtain walls, gatehouses, bastions, gu
beacon.civicRole: The Section 1 signal landmark; citizens watch it for alerts, public notices, and Conjunction response.
storyHooks: a signal is interpreted before officials arrive
crowds block the approach
the beacon reveals which district reacts first

seatOfPower.name: Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Civic authority made visible through courts, archives, command platforms, and public legitimacy.
seatOfPower.visualRole: The district-13 seat of power: courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, and the visible apparatus of government. It is not a faction HQ; it is the city's central civic authority. Built for Skyrend Peak's raptor-like avian folk population, it uses Granite, Slate, Cedar, Glass, Canvas across Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces; water context: dry city ground; magic expression: Beast Bonding,
seatOfPower.civicRole: District 13 authority center for petitions, archives, judgments, and city-scale decisions; not a faction headquarters.
storyHooks: a sealed record contradicts public belief
a petition exposes citywide risk
command platforms become crowd-facing stages

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 / Fortress / Border Wall must preserve Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Border Wall Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, and Watch Bastion Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S2 / Heavy Industry / Foundry must preserve Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Great Forge, Smoke-Stack Monument, and Crucible Hall Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S3 / Military Command must preserve Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Woven Banners Monument, and Standard Tower Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S4 / Observatory / Astronomy must preserve Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, Star Observatory, Star Navigation Monument, and Celestial Court Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S5 / Prison / Containment must preserve Prison / Containment Civic Hall, Containment Keep, Mist Veils Monument, and Judgment Yard Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show Skyrend Peak as a raptor-like avian folk city using Granite, Slate, Cedar, Glass, Canvas across Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces.
Keep Raptor Windspire Eyrie of the First Flight as the skyline-scale Wonder, Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat as central D13 authority, and Border Wall Beacon as the public signal landmark.
Use section landmark distribution to avoid flattening the city: Fortress / Border Wall, Heavy Industry / Foundry, Military Command, Observatory / Astronomy, Prison / Containment.
Water and terrain must follow source context: dry city ground; Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces.
requiredVisualElements: Raptor Windspire Eyrie of the First Flight
elementsToAvoid: invented district-specific landmarks not supported by sidecars
replacing proper anchor names with generic summaries
collapsing five section spines into one repeated street type
